content: Caffeine works by blocking adenosine receptors, which normally signal drowsiness.
The liver enzyme CYP1A2 breaks down caffeine into theabromine, affecting its effects.
The AHR gene affects how quickly caffeine is broken down, influencing sensitivity.
Researchers at Harvard found that some people have genes that affect their dopamine response to caffeine.
Blocking adenosine receptors weakens them over time, suggesting a caffeine cycling can enhance fat loss.
